name: Junior Cook
abstract: Herman "Junior" Cook (July 22, 1934 – February 3, 1992) was an American hard bop tenor saxophone player.

concerts.label: Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Freddie Hubbard Sextet

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: So I put one together. I got Junior Cook and Ron Matthews and Stafford James, and then we got Woody Shaw. So it was the Louis Hayes-Junior Cook Quintet featuring Woody Shaw.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louis Hayes interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Davis
about_quotes.evidence: I listened to a lot of Blue Note records, Lee Morgan and Hank Mobley and Horace Silver, Junior Cook and Blue Mitchell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 15, 2017

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...you even got Junior Cook . . . John Stubblefield, Joe Ford, so you brought in- [INTERVIEWEE] The older guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I used to see Junior Cook and Blue Mitchell with Chick Corea and Gene… they were part of Horace's band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: one of my unsung heroes, besides Junior Cook, is the other tenor player from the Bronx.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph LaLama
about_quotes.evidence: I love Junior Cook. I wasn’t trying to blow any — I couldn’t, because I was a kid.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph LaLama interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 23, 2010
